channel.title: World News, Today World News, Latest International News, World Breaking News, Trending News of World - Times of India
channel.description: World News Today: Read the latest world news headlines, Current International breaking news world wide. In depth analysis and top news headlines world wide.

image.title: World News, Today World News, Latest International News, World Breaking News, Trending News of World - Times of India

item.title: Meet Vaibhav Bhaskar, an Indian-origin student who broke Florida's high school record with 11.99 GPA
item.description: Achieving a stunning 11.99 GPA, Indian-origin Vaibhav Bhaskar has rewritten the record books for Florida high schools. His extraordinary performance sparked a critical review of grading practices, acadmic burnout of students. Vaibhav said he was not burned out but tired and now it felt surreal that suddenly he did not have anything to do after his high school graduation.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Scientists listen for alien signals on K2-18b but detect only cosmic silence
item.dc:creator: TOI Science Desk

item.title: MIT built a 300-gram robot that flies through the sky, dives underwater and takes off again, just like a diving bird
item.description: The Flapping-wing Aerial-aquatic Vehicle (FAAV), developed by engineers at MIT and EPFL, is a 300-gram robot designed to mimic the diving birds like the Atlantic puffin. It is the first machine capable of completing the full cycle of swimming, diving, and launching back into the sky using its wings rather than separate propellers or legs.
item.dc:creator: TOI Science Desk

item.title: Mercury is the closest planet to the Sun yet Venus is hotter; thanks to a runaway greenhouse effect
item.dc:creator: TOI Science Desk

item.title: Jeff Bezos's wife could not afford a prom dress growing up. Now she and the Amazon founder are...
item.description: In a significant philanthropic move, Lauren Sanchez and Jeff Bezos have pledged $34 million towards cutting-edge sustainable textile research. This funding aims to advance the creation of biodegradable fibers and plastic-free synthetic silk. For Sanchez, this initiative is particularly meaningful, having faced personal hardships in her youth. The couple also toasted their first wedding anniversary aboard their luxurious superyacht, amplifying their commitment to environmental conservation and community support.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Pluto's moon Charon may have once spun once every 14 hours, and its ancient mountains still record how it slowed down
item.description: New research suggests Charon's oldest landscapes preserve evidence of early despinning. Tectonic features in Oz Terra indicate a period of faster rotation for the moon. These structures resemble landforms created by crustal compression, which is consistent with despinning models. Scientists estimate Charon's initial rotation period was approximately fourteen hours. This ancient process likely occurred before other major geological events reshaped its surface.
item.dc:creator: TOI Science Desk

item.title: Beheading of Indian-origin motel manager in Dallas: Prosecutors say they won't seek death penalty against accused
item.description: Prosecutors have opted against seeking the death penalty for Yordanis Cobos-Martinez in the grim case of motel manager Chandra Nagamallaiah's murder. Allegations point to a fierce quarrel that culminated in Martinez allegedly beheading Nagamallaiah, with harrowing footage documenting the act. Should he be found guilty, Martinez will face life behind bars without the possibility of parole.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: An Australian cyclist accidentally trapped a 2-metre venomous eastern brown snake in her bike chain, then it bit her
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Did Kushner, Witkoff profit from Middle East war? What Iran claimed in private message to Vance
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Iceland heats every home using energy from volcanoes, and almost all of its electricity comes from renewable sources, making it one of the world's cleanest energy systems
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: 211 patients undergoing chemo evacuated: US strikes near Iran cancer hospital for children - video
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: More than 60 artisans have spent over two decades building a castle like it's 1230; it's still not finished
item.description: A French historical experiment began building a medieval castle in 1997. Skilled craftspeople use only hand tools and traditional methods for construction. This remarkable project has become a major tourist attraction and research center. Workers learn by studying ruins and experimenting with historical techniques. The castle remains unfinished after over twenty-five years of dedicated work.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Guam kingfishers vanished from their home after invasive snakes devastated their species: Now scientists are restoring them with four new chicks
item.description: The Guam Kingfisher, known as 'sihek', is extinct in the wild due to invasive brown tree snakes. These birds played vital roles in the island's ecosystem, controlling insect populations. Their disappearance has led to increased spider numbers and thinner forests on Guam. Conservation centers now house the remaining 140 birds, aiming for eventual reintroduction. Efforts focus on breeding pairs to save this species from complete extinction.
item.dc:creator: TOI Science Desk

item.title: Quote of the day by the Dalai Lama: "Good human qualities… honesty, sincerity, a good heart, cannot be bought with money, nor can they be produced by machines, but…"
item.description: Inner human qualities like honesty and a good heart are essential. These qualities cannot be bought with money or produced by machines. They are cultivated by the mind and form the essence of humanity. Visible achievements and material wealth do not define true character. Genuine human connection relies on these deeply ingrained values.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: A woman with a Stanford PhD in mathematics has been claimed to have cracked the lottery algorithm after winning the lottery four times and collecting more than $20 million
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: A 67-million-year-old T. rex discovered on a South Dakota ranch has sold for $50.1 million, becoming the most expensive dinosaur fossil ever auctioned
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Around 2 billion years ago, Oklo became a natural nuclear reactor beneath Africa, and its radioactive remains are still there
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Quote of the day by Kate Middleton: "People often ask me why I am so interested in the mental health of children and young people. The answer is…" - a powerful message on childhood mental health and why early support can shape a lifetime
item.description: Kate Middleton emphasizes that every child deserves the best possible start in life. This involves nurturing emotional well-being, not just addressing crises. Early years are vital for developing trust and security in children. Children often show distress indirectly, requiring adult awareness and patience. Providing emotional foundations helps children face life's challenges with confidence.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: 'Act now': Indian teen Romanch Mahajan's father urges NYC to ban horse carriages after son's death
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Ukraine gets new PM: Parliament approves Serhii Koretskyi amid cabinet shuffle
item.description: Ukraine's parliament approved Serhii Koretskyi as the new prime minister on Thursday. This appointment follows the resignation of former prime minister Yulia Svyrydenko. President Zelenskyy cited Koretskyi's energy sector experience for winter preparations. The government reshuffle includes other senior officials and aims to refresh leadership. Mykhailo Fedorov's dismissal as defense minister sparked protests and criticism.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk
